description: Tier rules for the Plures AI Ecosystem Explorer. Tiers: Nascent < Emerging < Established, per dimension and per track. Talent and policy thresholds were calibrated on the first real snapshot (2026-07) and are FROZEN with the justifications below until a versioned methodology revision. Attention rules remain provisional pending sufficient A1 data.

calibration_status.policy: calibrated on snapshot 2026-07, frozen (rules are definitional maps of curated codings)
calibration_status.attention: PENDING - first GDELT collection returned partial data (13/44 query sets, most empty); rules stay provisional and tiers stay null until at least one full A1 collection

mainstream.comment: t1_share = AI works / total works, rolling 3y. Compared against a FROZEN reference share rather than the live median, so tiers cannot churn month-to-month from denominator drift.
mainstream.reference_justification: G20 median share in snapshot 2026-07 (DE=0.02033), nearly identical to the US share (0.02051), making the threshold readable as 'AI occupies at least as large a slice of national research output as it does in the median G20 country / the US'.
mainstream.nascent_justification: 0.6x reference (=1.22%) sits in a visible gap in the 2026-07 distribution between ZA (0.96%) and MX (1.37%), separating the low cluster (AR, BR, ZA, JP*) from the middle.
mainstream.known_artifact: JP share (0.15%) is implausible: Japan's total-works denominator is inflated by J-STAGE bulk indexing. JP's mainstream talent tier is unreliable until the collector filters type:article on both numerator and denominator (queued fix). Similar inflation affects ID in the opposite direction (local-journal AI volume).

frontier.comment: t2 = frontier works since 2022; t3 = curated orgs + university groups. Established requires BOTH research output and organized field-building; a country can be Emerging on either alone (e.g. AR: 1 paper but 3 active groups; CN: 151 papers but 3 orgs).
frontier.established_justification: t2>=100 captures the top quartile of the 2026-07 G20 distribution (US 1498, GB 403, CA 224, DE 159, CN 151, IN 116 above; FR 84 below). t3>=5 = a field, not an outpost. Yields Established: US, GB, CA, DE, IN - CN excluded by design (research without civil society is the KR/CN gap the explorer exists to surface).
frontier.nascent_justification: t2<=20 sits in the distribution gap between ZA (19) and RU (28); t3<=1 means no organized community. Yields Nascent: MX, TR, SA. AR escapes Nascent via its 3 active groups despite 1 paper - correct: field-building exists there ahead of research.

attention.comment: Within-country only (GDELT timelinevol is % of the country's monitored coverage). ratio = mean of last 12 monthly values / mean of prior 12. If A1 data is absent the tier is null with insufficient_data=true - missing is never zero. PROVISIONAL: first collection (2026-07) returned 13/44 query sets before the time budget, and most frontier-track non-English queries returned empty timelines - thresholds below are uncalibrated guesses.

mainstream.comment: Definitional map of the curated P1 activity_level coding (low/medium/high), which already synthesizes OECD initiative count, binding instruments, dedicated bodies, and 2025-26 recency.

frontier.comment: From curated P2 checklist score (0-5). 0-1 = at most a generic strategy (Nascent: AR, RU); 2-3 = engaged but not committed (Emerging: ID, MX, SA, TR, ZA); 4-5 = multiple concrete frontier commitments (Established: the rest). Cut points match the natural clustering of the 2026-07 coding.
